name: Arantzazu Sanctuary and Udana Pass Loop
name_original: Subida al Santuario de Arantzazu — circular desde Zegama
description: This difficult 48.2-mile road cycling route from Zegama climbs 6014 feet to the Sanctuary of Arantzazu through the Basque Mountains.
region_intro: Road cycling around Zegama offers diverse terrain within the Basque Country, characterized by its proximity to the Aizkorri-Aratz Natural Park. The region features dramatic limestone mountains, dense beech forests, and verdant valleys, providing varied elevation profiles for cyclists. Routes often traverse mountain passes, offering significant climbs and scenic backdrops. This landscape transitions from steep slopes to high grazing lands, creating a rich visual experience for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Zegama The most popular road cycling route is Arantzazu Sanctuary and Udana Pass Loop, a 48.2 miles (77.6 km) trail that takes 4 hours 27 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gains through mountainous landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Atagoiti – View of Aztiria from Mutiloa loop from Zegama, a moderate 30.3 miles (48.8 km) path. This route offers views of the Atagoiti Valley and the surrounding Basque Mountains. Local road cyclists also love the Mandubia Summit – Ormaiztegi Viaduct loop from Zegama, a 26.0 miles (41.9 km) trail leading through mountain passes and valleys, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Zegama is defined by limestone mountains, dense beech forests, and scenic valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from challenging ascents to more moderate rides. The routes in Zegama are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 7,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Zegama's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Zegama?
A: There are over 70 road cycling routes around Zegama, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy rides to challenging routes with significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain and landscapes will I encounter while road cycling in Zegama?
A: Road cycling in Zegama offers incredibly diverse terrain. You'll experience challenging ascents through the dramatic limestone mountains of the Aizkorri-Aratz Natural Park, ride through dense beech forests like Iturrigorri, and enjoy scenic stretches through verdant valleys and high grazing lands such as the Campas de Urbia. Expect varied elevation profiles, including mountain passes and steep slopes, providing a rich visual experience.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Zegama offers routes for various ability levels. While many routes feature significant climbs, there are 20 easy routes and 32 moderate routes available. These often traverse more gentle valleys or offer less demanding ascents, allowing for a more relaxed cycling experience.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass by significant landmarks. You could cycle towards the spectacular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4857424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arantzazu Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;, perched over a deep gorge, or explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/837300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Adrián Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient pass that is part of the Camino de Santiago. The region also features several mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Udana Pass Ascent&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3405468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atagoiti&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Zegama?
A: The road cycling routes around Zegama are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the stunning natural landscapes, the challenging yet rewarding climbs, and the variety of routes that cater to different fitness levels. The blend of dramatic mountains, lush forests, and cultural landmarks makes for a memorable experience.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Zegama?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Zegama are designed as loops. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1033160396/arantzazu-sanctuary-and-udana-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arantzazu Sanctuary and Udana Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38218767" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atagoiti – View of Aztiria from Mutiloa loop from Zegama&lt;/a&gt;. These circular routes allow you to start and finish in the same location, often Zegama itself.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Zegama?
A: The Basque Country generally offers pleasant cycling conditions from spring through autumn. While specific weather can vary, these seasons typically provide milder temperatures and less precipitation, making them ideal for tackling the region's mountain passes and scenic roads. Always check the local forecast before heading out, especially when planning ascents in higher elevations.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Zegama is known for its challenging mountain ascents. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1033160396/arantzazu-sanctuary-and-udana-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arantzazu Sanctuary and Udana Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains, with some routes offering over 6,000 feet of climbing. These difficult routes traverse mountainous landscapes and are perfect for experienced cyclists seeking a demanding ride.

Q: Can I find routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain around Zegama provides numerous opportunities for panoramic views. Routes often climb to mountain passes and summits, offering expansive vistas of the Basque Mountains, the Atagoiti Valley, and the surrounding natural park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5571733" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Otzaurte&lt;/a&gt; pass, for example, is known for its scenic beauty.

Q: Is there a visitor center in Zegama for more information about the area?
A: Yes, the Aizkorriko Ataria Visitor Center is located in Zegama. It provides valuable insights into the cultural and natural features of the Aizkorri-Aratz Natural Park, making it an excellent resource for planning your outdoor explorations, including road cycling routes.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling options or connections to other areas?
A: While many routes are loops, the region also features connections like the Legorreta-Zegama bike lane, which links several towns and historical sites. Additionally, the Urola Greenway offers sections with panoramic views, providing options for longer rides or linking up with other regional cycling networks.
tips: Atagoiti is located in Basque Country. From Ormaiztegi, the Atagoiti is 9.5 km long. Along this distance 322 altimeters are bridged. The average slope of the rise is, therefore, 3.4%.

The Sanctuary of Arantzazu, hanging over ravines and built on rocky outcrops in a wild and natural area, in the municipality of Oñati, survived three major fires in its long existence (1553, 1622 and 1834), having to be rebuilt as many times .

Udana from Oñati is a climb in the Basque Country region. It has a length of 6.9 kilometers and an ascent of 270 vertical meters with a slope of 3.9% on average. The top of the ascent is located at 515 meters above sea level.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the 'Climb to the Sanctuary of Arantzazu — circular from Zegama' route?
faqs.answer: This is a difficult road cycling route, classified as a 'Hard road ride'. It demands very good fitness due to its significant elevation gain of 1,830 meters over 77.6 kilometers. Cyclists should be prepared for a long and strenuous effort.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given its distance and elevation gain, completing this route typically takes well over five hours of riding time for experienced cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features mostly well-paved surfaces that are generally easy to ride. However, some segments may include unpaved or difficult sections, characteristic of a challenging mountain road ride in the Basque region.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the challenging Atagoiti and Udana Pass Ascent mountain passes. The journey culminates at the stunning Sanctuary of Arantzazu, a significant architectural and religious landmark dramatically situated on a cliff edge.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Zegama?
faqs.answer: As the route is circular from Zegama, you can typically find parking within the town to begin your ride. Specific parking areas for cyclists are usually available near the town center or designated public parking lots.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder and more stable. Summer can also be pleasant, but be prepared for potentially warmer temperatures on the climbs. Always check the weather forecast before you go, especially for mountain conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Aizkorri-Aratz Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Aizkorri-Aratz Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked routes, and be mindful of the natural environment.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a demanding road cycling route, it is not typically considered 'dog-friendly' in the traditional sense of a hiking trail. If you plan to bring a dog, they would need to be able to keep pace with a bicycle or be carried safely, which is generally not recommended for such a strenuous ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll traverse the picturesque Basque Mountains in Gipuzkoa, specifically within the Aizkorri-Aratz Natural Park. Expect stunning natural beauty, including lush green forests, rugged limestone peaks, and deep valleys with streams. The dramatic setting of the Sanctuary of Arantzazu is a highlight.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other trails, including the [CIMA GP01] Aranzazu, E3 Gaztaren Ibilbidea: Segura - Mirandaola, and the Camino de Santiago Vasco del Interior.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for this challenging road climb?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and elevation, you should bring a well-maintained road bike with appropriate gearing for steep climbs. Essential gear includes a helmet, sufficient water and nutrition, repair kit, spare tubes, and layers of clothing suitable for changing mountain weather. A GPS device with the route loaded is also highly recommended.
